truthAnchors: DELIGHT IN THE LORD, AND HE WILL GIVE YOU THE DESIRES OF YOUR HEART.
BE STILL, AND KNOW THAT I AM GOD.
MY GRACE IS SUFFICIENT FOR YOU, FOR MY POWER IS MADE PERFECT IN WEAKNESS.
CAST ALL YOUR ANXIETY ON HIM BECAUSE HE CARES FOR YOU.
ABIDE IN ME, AND I IN YOU. APART FROM ME YOU CAN DO NOTHING.
THE STEADFAST LOVE OF THE LORD NEVER CEASES; HIS MERCIES ARE NEW EVERY MORNING.

thread.title: SERMON SERIES: THE SECRET PLACE
thread.summary: Outline research for the next series on hidden prayer and the inner life.

extraFeed.label: DISCIPLERS' MEETING · 5/31
extraFeed.text: With Joel Tillis. Three goals — consistency, change, connection — plus a fourth C: comprehension (disciples must be able to verbalize understanding). Aim ~50–60% dialogue, weave application questions through the lesson, and have disciples teach to prove retention.

extraFeed.label: SPIRITUAL GROWTH SEMINAR · 6/9
extraFeed.text: With Ben Stanton. Works of the flesh vs. fruit of the Spirit — the 'two dogs' picture, new nature as the lion. Yielding to the Spirit and the Word as the engine of growth; framework materials to be updated and shared with a women's group.

extraFeed.label: PREMARITAL COUNSELING · Jonas & Maliah (6/4, 6/11)
extraFeed.text: Two sessions: roles and reverence (Eph 5, 1 Tim 3), covering offenses and fighting well, in-law boundaries, daily/weekly spiritual rhythms, purity accountability, and a zero-based budget grounded in stewardship and contentment. Couple's homework due session 3.

extraFeed.label: APPOINTMENT · Matthew Reigle (6/4)
extraFeed.text: Career discernment anchored to values and spiritual growth: a bounded weekly planning slot, informational talks with experienced managers, a clear job-evaluation question set. Marriage: listen and enter his wife's frustrations before solving; build shared, playful family time.

extraFeed.label: ROMANS 2 · LESSON 17 (6/11)
extraFeed.text: What the Bible says about race: all humans in God's image (Gen 1:26-27), descended from Adam and Noah's sons; the law given to Jews, not Gentiles. Unity in Christ over racial division; evangelism over pride.

extraFeed.label: JOEL TILLIS · MINISTRY & SOCIAL MEDIA (5/31)
extraFeed.text: Danger of building a following vs. shepherding the local flock — pastors as ER doctors for acute spiritual needs, balancing deep doctrine with practical care.

extraFeed.label: KJV THOUGHTS (6/4)
extraFeed.text: An open musing on whether to modernize archaic language ('restoreth' → 'restores') for accessibility, and how much weight to give the original tongues. No action yet.

spokes.title: THE WORK
spokes.subtitle: Shepherding — leading the church and staff

thread.title: STAFF & PASTORAL CARE LOG
thread.summary: Follow-ups from Sunday — staff one-on-ones, two hospital visits, a grief call.

extraFeed.label: RON RAINS · LEADERSHIP & MINISTRY EVAL (6/11)
extraFeed.text: Finances, accountability, and purchasing controls: end personal use of church cards, stand up an Amazon Business account (personal/business/benefits split), remove the church card from the personal account. Member-care reimbursement protocol needed; Canva/Prime Timer brand consistency; explore Ben building a member-care app.

extraFeed.label: CHURCH LEADERSHIP STRATEGY (5/31)
extraFeed.text: A brief pre-Sunday sync and prayer with connection-group leaders and deacons; high standards for younger men without inflating egos; exploring a bus ministry and better outreach lead-capture (automated email/text — consult Sammy, engage Ben).

extraFeed.label: CHURCH EVENT PLANNING REVIEW (6/11)
extraFeed.text: With Jess. Decoration/budget confusion traced to a leader's lack of protocol, not malice. Need clear spending guidelines and accountability for member-care activity; Jess to review and approve decoration plans going forward.

extraFeed.label: CHURCH COMMUNICATION STRATEGY (6/8)
extraFeed.text: Jess to own church-wide communication and event triage: publish summer outreach dates in the bulletin, attend staff monthly, set a recurring calendar review with Amy, and align Brother John on outreach scheduling.

extraFeed.label: DOCUMENT CREATION PLANNING (6/11)
extraFeed.text: With Jess. Draft a counseling-scheduling protocol and suggested request language (submit ideas a couple months ahead); word it carefully to avoid sounding self-serving.

extraFeed.label: KIDS MUSIC CAMP REVIEW (6/4)
extraFeed.text: For next year: send songs and sheet music to kids ahead of time, standardize vocal-instruction cues with visuals (diction), and confirm each teacher's plan. Strong devotional moments on vowel sounds and worship.

extraFeed.label: IRONMEN: D-DAY (6/6)
extraFeed.text: Camaraderie and unity as a D-Day picture — personal relationship with Jesus, mutual support, timing and sacrifice, and the strength of small, consistent, group efforts.

extraFeed.label: STUDENT APPEAL STRATEGY (6/10)
extraFeed.text: With Jess. A student at 75 demerits, affecting her ability to pay for the semester. Verify the count and the written policy; encourage her to own her past, document her growth, and write an appeal.

extraFeed.label: SCHOOL DISCIPLINARY REVIEW (6/10)
extraFeed.text: With Jess re: her daughter — suspended for crossing the 75-demerit threshold, which caused an over-absence; one absence already retracted by Mrs. Carter. Frame it positively for her, acknowledge the school's accommodations, and follow up with Mrs. Carter.
